text.title: Gcp Billing Cost Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the gcp-billing-cost methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Cost analysis limited to Cloud Console UI; no joinable fact table.
antipattern.root-cause: BQ billing export not enabled on billing account.
antipattern.fix: Enable detailed-usage + pricing tables export to BQ; backfill what's available.

antipattern.symptom: Overspend discovered at month-end invoice, not during the month.
antipattern.fix: Create budget alerts at 50/90/100% per project; route via Pub/Sub.

antipattern.symptom: Cost attribution stuck at project level; can't allocate to teams.
antipattern.fix: Enforce labels via org policy + automated label backfill scripts.

antipattern.symptom: Baseline compute spend 30–55% higher than necessary.
antipattern.root-cause: No committed-use discount evaluation in the last 90 days.
antipattern.fix: Quarterly CUD review using Recommender; commit on stable baseline.

antipattern.symptom: Idle Cloud Run, unattached IPs, oversized Cloud SQL accumulate cost.
antipattern.root-cause: Recommender's idle / oversized recommendations are not actioned.
antipattern.fix: Monthly sweep of Recommender output; track action rate as KPI.
